x-enumeration-literals.name: Due Method
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Interest remains payable when due, not compounded.

Comment
Example: Due Method
Loan Principal: ₹100,000
Interest Rate: 10% per year

Scenario: Borrower does not pay interest for 1 year.

Calculation:
Interest accrued = ₹100,000 × 10% = ₹10,000

At year-end:
Principal remains ₹100,000
Interest due separately = ₹10,000
Total liability = ₹110,000 (no compounding)

The unpaid interest stays as a separate due amount. It does not generate further interest.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Capitalization Method
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Unpaid interest is added to principal, compounding liability.

Comment
Example: Capitalization Method
Loan Principal: ₹100,000
Interest Rate: 10% per year

Scenario: Borrower does not pay interest for 1 year.

Calculation:
Interest accrued = ₹100,000 × 10% = ₹10,000

At year-end:
Interest is capitalized (added to principal) → New principal = ₹110,000
Next years interest = ₹110,000 × 10% = ₹11,000
Total liability after 2 years = ₹121,000 (compounded)
